mitigations.name: Application Developer Guidance
mitigations.comment: A future release of D3FEND will define a taxonomy of Source Code Hardening Techniques.

mitigations.name: Active Directory Configuration
mitigations.comment: M1015 scope is broad, touches on an wide variety of techniques in D3FEND.

d3fend.name: Domain Trust Policy
d3fend.definition: Restricting inter-domain trust by modifying domain configuration.

d3fend.name: Authentication Cache Invalidation
d3fend.definition: Removing tokens or credentials from an authentication cache to prevent further user associated account accesses.

d3fend.name: User Account Permissions
d3fend.definition: Restricting a user account's access to resources.

mitigations.name: Vulnerability Scanning
mitigations.comment: Future D3FEND releases will model the scanning and inventory domains.

mitigations.name: User Training
mitigations.comment: Modeling user training is  outside the scope of D3FEND.

d3fend.name: Local File Permissions
d3fend.definition: Local file permissions is the systematic process of defining, implementing, and managing access control policies that dictate user permissions for accessing files on a local system through the configuration of operating system functionality.

d3fend.name: System Call Filtering
d3fend.definition: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.

d3fend.name: System Configuration Permissions
d3fend.definition: Restricting system configuration modifications to a specific user or group of users.

mitigations.name: Threat Intelligence Program
mitigations.comment: Establishing and running a Threat Intelligence Program is outside the scope of D3FEND.

mitigations.name: SSL/TLS Inspection
mitigations.comment: D3FEND models this as an infrastructure dependency to support D3-NTA.

d3fend.name: Network Traffic Analysis
d3fend.definition: Analyzing intercepted or summarized computer network traffic to detect unauthorized activity.

mitigations.name: Restrict Web-Based Content
mitigations.comment: M1021 scope is broad, touches on an wide variety of techniques in d3fend.

d3fend.name: Inbound Traffic Filtering
d3fend.definition: Restricting network traffic originating from untrusted networks destined towards a private host or enclave.

d3fend.name: DNS Denylisting
d3fend.definition: Blocking DNS Network Traffic based on criteria such as IP address, domain name, or DNS query type.

d3fend.name: DNS Allowlisting
d3fend.definition: Permitting only approved domains and their subdomains to be resolved.

d3fend.name: Network Traffic Analysis
d3fend.definition: Analyzing intercepted or summarized computer network traffic to detect unauthorized activity.

d3fend.name: File Analysis
d3fend.definition: File Analysis is an analytic process to determine a file's status. For example: virus, trojan, benign, malicious, trusted, unauthorized, sensitive, etc.

d3fend.name: Outbound Traffic Filtering
d3fend.definition: Restricting network traffic originating from a private host or enclave destined towards untrusted networks.

d3fend.name: URL Analysis
d3fend.definition: Determining if a URL is benign or malicious by analyzing the URL or its components.

d3fend.name: Local File Permissions
d3fend.definition: Local file permissions is the systematic process of defining, implementing, and managing access control policies that dictate user permissions for accessing files on a local system through the configuration of operating system functionality.

d3fend.name: System Configuration Permissions
d3fend.definition: Restricting system configuration modifications to a specific user or group of users.

d3fend.name: Bootloader Authentication
d3fend.definition: Cryptographically authenticating the bootloader software before system boot.

d3fend.name: Process Segment Execution Prevention
d3fend.definition: Preventing execution of any address in a memory region other than the code segment.

d3fend.name: System Call Filtering
d3fend.definition: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.

d3fend.name: Driver Load Integrity Checking
d3fend.definition: Ensuring the integrity of drivers loaded during initialization of the operating system.

d3fend.name: Local Account Monitoring
d3fend.definition: Analyzing local user accounts to detect unauthorized activity.

d3fend.name: Domain Account Monitoring
d3fend.definition: Monitoring the existence of or changes to Domain User Accounts.

d3fend.name: Strong Password Policy
d3fend.definition: Modifying system configuration to increase password strength.

d3fend.name: One-time Password
d3fend.definition: A one-time password is valid for only one user authentication.

d3fend.name: Strong Password Policy
d3fend.definition: Modifying system configuration to increase password strength.

d3fend.name: Platform Hardening
d3fend.definition:
Hardening components of a Platform with the intention of making them more difficult to exploit.

Platforms includes components such as:
* BIOS UEFI Subsystems
* Hardware security devices such as Trusted Platform Modules
* Boot process logic or code
* Kernel software components

mitigations.name: Remote Data Storage
mitigations.comment: IT disaster recovery plans are outside the current scope of D3FEND.

d3fend.name: Encrypted Tunnels
d3fend.definition: Encrypted encapsulation of routable network traffic.

d3fend.name: Inbound Traffic Filtering
d3fend.definition: Restricting network traffic originating from untrusted networks destined towards a private host or enclave.

d3fend.name: Broadcast Domain Isolation
d3fend.definition: Broadcast isolation restricts the number of computers a host can contact on their LAN.

d3fend.name: Inbound Session Volume Analysis
d3fend.definition: Analyzing inbound network session or connection attempt volume.

d3fend.name: Inbound Traffic Filtering
d3fend.definition: Restricting network traffic originating from untrusted networks destined towards a private host or enclave.

d3fend.name: Network Traffic Analysis
d3fend.definition: Analyzing intercepted or summarized computer network traffic to detect unauthorized activity.

d3fend.name: Outbound Traffic Filtering
d3fend.definition: Restricting network traffic originating from a private host or enclave destined towards untrusted networks.

d3fend.name: Multi-factor Authentication
d3fend.definition: Requiring proof of two or more pieces of evidence in order to authenticate a user.

d3fend.name: Executable Allowlisting
d3fend.definition: Using a digital signature to authenticate a file before opening.

d3fend.name: Executable Denylisting
d3fend.definition: Blocking the execution of files on a host in accordance with defined application policy rules.

d3fend.name: IO Port Restriction
d3fend.definition: Limiting access to computer input/output (IO) ports to restrict unauthorized devices.

mitigations.name: Limit Access to Resource Over Network

d3fend.name: Network Isolation
d3fend.definition: Network Isolation techniques prevent network hosts from accessing non-essential system network resources.

mitigations.name: Account Use Policies
mitigations.comment: D3-AZET may be related (is potentially related though not called out in ATT&CK definition.)

d3fend.name: Account Locking
d3fend.definition: The process of temporarily disabling user accounts on a system or domain.

d3fend.name: Authentication Cache Invalidation
d3fend.definition: Removing tokens or credentials from an authentication cache to prevent further user associated account accesses.

d3fend.name: Authentication Event Thresholding
d3fend.definition: Collecting authentication events, creating a baseline user profile, and determining whether authentication events are consistent with the baseline profile.

d3fend.name: Network Isolation
d3fend.definition: Network Isolation techniques prevent network hosts from accessing non-essential system network resources.

d3fend.name: Executable Allowlisting
d3fend.definition: Using a digital signature to authenticate a file before opening.

d3fend.name: Process Segment Execution Prevention
d3fend.definition: Preventing execution of any address in a memory region other than the code segment.

d3fend.name: Driver Load Integrity Checking
d3fend.definition: Ensuring the integrity of drivers loaded during initialization of the operating system.

d3fend.name: Executable Denylisting
d3fend.definition: Blocking the execution of files on a host in accordance with defined application policy rules.

d3fend.name: Application Configuration Hardening
d3fend.definition: Modifying an application's configuration to reduce its attack surface.

d3fend.name: System File Analysis
d3fend.definition: Monitoring system files such as authentication databases, configuration files, system logs, and system executables for modification or tampering.

d3fend.name: User Data Transfer Analysis
d3fend.definition: Analyzing the amount of data transferred by a user.

d3fend.name: Web Session Activity Analysis
d3fend.definition: Monitoring changes in user web session behavior by comparing current web session activity to a baseline behavior profile or a catalog of predetermined malicious behavior.

d3fend.name: Resource Access Pattern Analysis
d3fend.definition: Analyzing the resources accessed by a user to identify unauthorized activity.

d3fend.name: Job Function Access Pattern Analysis
d3fend.definition: Detecting anomalies in user access patterns by comparing user access activity to behavioral profiles that categorize users by role such as job title, function, department.

d3fend.name: User Geolocation Logon Pattern Analysis
d3fend.definition: Monitoring geolocation data of user logon attempts and comparing it to a baseline user behavior profile to identify anomalies in logon location.

d3fend.name: Authorization Event Thresholding
d3fend.definition: Collecting authorization events, creating a baseline user profile, and determining whether authorization events are consistent with the baseline profile.

d3fend.name: Authentication Event Thresholding
d3fend.definition: Collecting authentication events, creating a baseline user profile, and determining whether authentication events are consistent with the baseline profile.

d3fend.name: Session Duration Analysis
d3fend.definition: Analyzing the duration of user sessions in order to detect unauthorized  activity.

d3fend.name: Encrypted Tunnels
d3fend.definition: Encrypted encapsulation of routable network traffic.

d3fend.name: Disk Encryption
d3fend.definition: Encrypting a hard disk partition to prevent cleartext access to a file system.

d3fend.name: File Encryption
d3fend.definition: Encrypting a file using a cryptographic key.

d3fend.name: Message Encryption
d3fend.definition: Encrypting a message body using a cryptographic key.

mitigations.name: Disable or Remove Feature or Program

d3fend.name: Application Configuration Hardening
d3fend.definition: Modifying an application's configuration to reduce its attack surface.

d3fend.name: System Call Filtering
d3fend.definition: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.

d3fend.name: Executable Denylisting
d3fend.definition: Blocking the execution of files on a host in accordance with defined application policy rules.

d3fend.name: Hardware-based Process Isolation
d3fend.definition: Preventing one process from writing to the memory space of another process through hardware based address manager implementations.

mitigations.name: Restrict Library Loading
mitigations.comment: D3-SCF is one possible way to filter library loading.

d3fend.name: System Call Filtering
d3fend.definition: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.

d3fend.name: Executable Allowlisting
d3fend.definition: Using a digital signature to authenticate a file before opening.

d3fend.name: Service Binary Verification
d3fend.definition: Analyzing changes in service binary files by comparing to a source of truth.

d3fend.name: Driver Load Integrity Checking
d3fend.definition: Ensuring the integrity of drivers loaded during initialization of the operating system.

d3fend.name: Bootloader Authentication
d3fend.definition: Cryptographically authenticating the bootloader software before system boot.

d3fend.name: TPM Boot Integrity
d3fend.definition: Assuring the integrity of a platform by demonstrating that the boot process starts from a trusted combination of hardware and software and continues until the operating system has fully booted and applications are running.  Sometimes called Static Root of Trust Measurement (STRM).

mitigations.name: Audit
mitigations.comment: M1047 scope is broad, touches on an wide variety of techniques in d3fend.

d3fend.name: Local Account Monitoring
d3fend.definition: Analyzing local user accounts to detect unauthorized activity.

d3fend.name: Domain Account Monitoring
d3fend.definition: Monitoring the existence of or changes to Domain User Accounts.

d3fend.name: System File Analysis
d3fend.definition: Monitoring system files such as authentication databases, configuration files, system logs, and system executables for modification or tampering.

mitigations.name: Application Isolation and Sandboxing
mitigations.comment: "Sandboxing" is often used to describe a detection environment which includes some forms of analysis (see D3-DA.)"  Many forms of isolation (e.g., quarantining) are more static in nature and simply limit software's access to system resources.

d3fend.name: Dynamic Analysis
d3fend.definition: Executing or opening a file in a synthetic "sandbox" environment to determine if the file is a malicious program or if the file exploits another program such as a document reader.

d3fend.name: System Call Filtering
d3fend.definition: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.

d3fend.name: Hardware-based Process Isolation
d3fend.definition: Preventing one process from writing to the memory space of another process through hardware based address manager implementations.

d3fend.name: Process Analysis
d3fend.definition: Process Analysis consists of observing a running application process and analyzing it to watch for certain behaviors or conditions which may indicate adversary activity. Analysis can occur inside of the process or through a third-party monitoring application. Examples include monitoring system and privileged calls, monitoring process initiation chains, and memory boundary allocations.

d3fend.name: File Hashing
d3fend.definition: Employing file hash comparisons to detect known malware.

d3fend.name: File Content Rules
d3fend.definition: Employing a pattern matching rule language to analyze the content of files.

d3fend.name: Inbound Traffic Filtering
d3fend.definition: Restricting network traffic originating from untrusted networks destined towards a private host or enclave.

d3fend.name: Exception Handler Pointer Validation
d3fend.definition: Validates that a referenced exception handler pointer is a valid exception handler.

d3fend.name: Shadow Stack Comparisons
d3fend.definition: Comparing a call stack in system memory with a shadow call stack maintained by the processor to determine unauthorized shellcode activity.

d3fend.name: Application Hardening
d3fend.definition: Application Hardening makes an executable application more resilient to a class of exploits which either introduce new code or execute unwanted existing code. These techniques may be applied at compile-time or on an application binary.

d3fend.name: Software Update
d3fend.definition: Replacing old software on a computer system component.

d3fend.name: System Call Filtering
d3fend.definition: Controlling access to local computer system resources with kernel-level capabilities.

mitigations.name: Data Backup
mitigations.comment: Comprehensive IT disaster recovery plans are outside the current scope of D3FEND.

d3fend.name: Certificate Pinning
d3fend.definition: Persisting either a server's X.509 certificate or their public key and comparing that to server's presented identity to allow for greater client confidence in the remote server's identity for SSL connections.

d3fend.name: Application Configuration Hardening
d3fend.definition: Modifying an application's configuration to reduce its attack surface.

d3fend.name: Decoy Environment
d3fend.definition: A Decoy Environment comprises hosts and networks for the purposes of deceiving an attacker.

d3fend.name: Decoy Object
d3fend.definition: A Decoy Object is created and deployed for the purposes of deceiving attackers.
